title: Daily Market Digest: May 15, 2026
subtitle: Markets retreat from records on inflation fears and rising yields

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Drop as Inflation Concerns Mount and Yields Climb
items.summary: U.S. equities pulled back from recent record highs on Friday, May 15, 2026, as investors grappled with persistent inflation fears, rising Treasury yields, and increased oil prices. Technology and AI-related stocks led the decline, reversing earlier optimism. The CBOE Volatility Index (VIX) surged, indicating heightened market uncertainty. Geopolitical tensions, including the ongoing war in Iran and the outcomes of the Trump-Xi summit, further influenced market sentiment.

items.headline: Figma Soars on Strong Earnings
items.summary: Figma Inc. ($FIG) surged 10.92% in premarket trading after exceeding first-quarter financial expectations and raising its full-year 2026 sales guidance.

items.headline: Nova Ltd. Jumps on Q1 Earnings Beat
items.summary: Nova Ltd. ($NVMI) saw its shares jump 10.4% following the release of better-than-anticipated first-quarter 2026 adjusted earnings of $2.33 per share, surpassing analyst estimates.

items.headline: Nvidia Leads Tech Sell-Off
items.summary: AI bellwether Nvidia ($NVDA) dropped 4.5% today, being the heaviest weight on the S&P 500, as traders rotated away from higher-risk technology assets amid inflation concerns.

items.headline: Applied Materials Slips Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Despite reporting better-than-expected second-quarter financial results and issuing strong third-quarter guidance, Applied Materials Inc. ($AMAT) shares fell 3.08%.

items.headline: Fed Leadership Transition and Hawkish Shift
items.summary: Jerome Powell's term as Federal Reserve Chair concludes today, with Kevin Warsh confirmed as his successor. This transition is seen as tilting the Fed incrementally more hawkish, especially following a rare 8-4 split vote at the April FOMC meeting, which has introduced uncertainty regarding future interest rate policy. Markets are currently pricing a 99.4% likelihood of rates remaining unchanged in June, but persistent inflation could prompt rate hikes.

items.headline: Inflation Worries Intensify as CPI Forecast Rises
items.summary: Inflation remains a significant concern, with April's Consumer Price Index (CPI) exceeding expectations at 3.8%. The Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland projects May's CPI to climb further to 4.2%. Rising energy costs, with WTI crude oil above $100 per barrel, are contributing to these inflationary pressures, impacting consumer spending and increasing borrowing costs for businesses.

items.headline: Technology Sector Experiences Significant Pullback
items.summary: After a period of strong gains, the technology sector experienced a sharp turnaround, leading market declines. AI and semiconductor stocks were particularly affected as investors moved away from higher-valuation assets amidst a climate of rising yields and inflation fears. Despite this, the tech sector is still anticipated to deliver the highest earnings growth among S&P 500 sectors in 2026 and 2027.

items.headline: Cisco Beats Q3 Estimates
items.summary: Cisco Systems ($CSCO) reported better-than-expected third-quarter earnings, with an adjusted EPS of $1.06 against estimates of $1.04, and revenue of $15.84 billion, surpassing the $15.56 billion expectation. The company also provided strong guidance for the fourth quarter, forecasting adjusted EPS of $1.16 to $1.18 per share.
